articles.title: Simple leukemia treatment change in India cuts early child deaths
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large clinical trial in India reported that using short steroid bursts instead of continuous dosing reduced early treatment-related deaths in children with acute B-cell precursor ALL. The report says this change did not reduce the chance of cure. The finding points to a practical protocol adjustment in pediatric leukemia care.

articles.title: Weight loss treatments linked obesity
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study of 229,000 adults with obesity but without diabetes found an association between GLP-1 receptor agonists and lower risk of obesity-related cancers. Compared with diet and exercise alone, use of these medicines was associated with a reported 41% lower overall cancer risk. As described, this is an observational association rather than proof of prevention.

articles.title: Study proposes reconsidering how histone deacetylase inhibitors work in cancer treatment
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers are challenging the long-standing view that HDAC inhibitors work mainly by blocking HDAC enzymes. The report suggests their anticancer effects may involve a different or broader mechanism than previously assumed. That could influence how these drugs are studied and optimized.

articles.title: Drug nearly doubles survival in advanced pancreatic cancer--how daraxonrasib overcame an 'undruggable' disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report describes daraxonrasib as a drug that nearly doubled survival in advanced pancreatic cancer in a major trial. It targets KRAS, a pathway long considered difficult to drug in this disease. The article frames the result as a potentially important advance for metastatic pancreatic cancer treatment.

articles.title: Some tumors eliminate healthy neighboring cells to grow, study reveals
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers report that some tumors may promote growth by eliminating nearby healthy cells, beyond simply evolving their own genomes. The work focuses on chromosomal instability and tumor aggressiveness in solid cancers. It adds a mechanistic view of how tumors may reshape surrounding tissue to their advantage.

articles.title: Researchers discover way to inhibit brain cancer's infiltration mechanism in glioblastoma
articles.summary_3_sentences: A research team identified a potential target involved in how glioblastoma infiltrates brain tissue. The article suggests blocking this mechanism could slow or limit spread of this highly aggressive cancer. The report is focused on experimental oncology findings rather than a ready treatment.

articles.title: Ozempic and similar weight-loss drugs linked to 30% lower breast cancer risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large study found that women taking GLP-1 drugs had a lower observed risk of breast cancer. Researchers described the association as promising but not definitive and said trials are being planned. The report does not establish that the drugs directly prevent breast cancer.

articles.title: Scientists finally crack an "undruggable" pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reports that daraxonrasib, a KRAS-targeting drug, nearly doubled survival in advanced pancreatic cancer in a major clinical trial. It presents the result as a breakthrough against a long-standing therapeutic challenge. The claim is tied to advanced disease treatment, not prevention.

articles.title: Cancer's favorite escape trick may actually make it easier to kill
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers report that when tumor cells lose MHC I, they may become more vulnerable to CD4+ T-cell responses. The finding challenges a common assumption about immune escape in cancer. It offers a mechanistic insight that could inform future immunotherapy strategies.

articles.title: A single protein may be holding back CAR T cancer therapy
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists identified NFIL3 as a factor that drives CAR-T-cell exhaustion in animal models. Disabling it helped engineered cells stay active longer and better control tumors in preclinical studies. The work is mechanistic and preclinical, not a human trial.

articles.title: One fat helped pancreatic cancer grow while another cut disease in half
articles.summary_3_sentences: In mice predisposed to pancreatic cancer, dietary fats had different effects on disease progression. The report says oleic acid accelerated tumor growth, while omega-3-rich fish oil slowed disease development. These are preclinical findings in mice and should not be taken as direct dietary advice for patients without further evidence.

articles.title: Why cancer spreads more in middle age than in old age
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study found melanoma spread was lowest in young animals, highest in middle age, and lower again in very old age. Researchers linked this pattern to a type of immune cell that may help keep cancer dormant. The work is preclinical and focused on age-related metastasis biology.

articles.title: Scientists say house cats could help unlock new cancer treatments for humans
articles.summary_3_sentences: A genomic analysis of nearly 500 cat tumors found similarities between feline, canine, and human cancers. The report highlights shared cancer-driving genes, including ones linked to aggressive breast cancers. It supports comparative oncology as a way to generate translational insights.

articles.title: Scientists supercharge natural killer cells to fight aggressive cancers
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that temporarily blocking two proteins boosted NK-cell activity against several difficult cancers. The evidence described is preclinical and includes leukemia, glioblastoma, kidney cancer, and triple-negative breast cancer models. The work suggests a possible strategy to strengthen innate immune cell therapies.

articles.title: New drugs could wipe out the "zombie cells" linked to cancer and aging
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers report a way to kill therapy-induced senescent cells by targeting GPX4, pushing them toward ferroptotic death. In mice, the approach reduced tumor size and improved survival. The findings are preclinical and relate to senescence-associated cancer persistence.

articles.title: A rare cancer-fighting plant compound has been decoded
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists identified two enzymes involved in producing mitraphylline, a rare plant compound with reported anticancer potential. The work solves a biosynthetic puzzle and may enable more sustainable production. It is a basic science advance, not a demonstration of clinical efficacy.

articles.title: Potential Rare Human-to-Human Transmission in a Hantavirus Outbreak
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report covers a hantavirus outbreak with concern about possible rare person-to-person transmission. It highlights the unusual transmission question rather than providing definitive proof. The article frames the event as an outbreak update with public health implications.

articles.title: Outbreak Updates: Measles in the US, Ebola in the DRC
articles.summary_3_sentences: This item summarizes current outbreak activity, including measles spread in the United States and Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. It emphasizes ongoing case growth and the need for continued public health response. The report is epidemiologic and situational rather than mechanistic.

articles.title: AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine passes first human trial
articles.summary_3_sentences: An AI-designed universal coronavirus vaccine was reported to be safe and well tolerated in its first human trial. It also generated immune responses against multiple coronaviruses, including SARS-CoV-2, SARS, and related bat viruses. The goal is broad family-level protection as coronaviruses evolve.

articles.title: Scientists sound the alarm as dangerous amoebas spread globally
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists warn that free-living amoebae are an underrecognized public health threat that can cause severe infections and shelter other microbes. The report links the concern to climate change and aging infrastructure that may widen exposure risks. It is a warning and synthesis piece rather than a single intervention study.

articles.title: First leishmaniasis vaccine enters phase one trial as disease spreads to US
articles.summary_3_sentences: A first-in-human phase 1 trial is being launched for a leishmaniasis vaccine. The article notes concern about the disease's spread into the United States and frames the vaccine as the first specifically developed for this skin disease. At this stage, the focus is safety testing.

articles.title: New study highlights maternal-fetal TORCH infection risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers assessed long-term trends in TORCH infections in a setting with previously limited data. The article highlights the risk that mild or silent maternal infections can pose to fetal development. It is an epidemiologic report centered on maternal-fetal infection burden.

articles.title: A $4 tongue swab test detects tuberculosis within 30 minutes
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article describes a low-cost tuberculosis test using a tongue swab that can produce results in about 30 minutes. The approach may help people who have trouble providing sputum or who have limited access to health care. It suggests a simpler point-of-care option for TB detection.

articles.title: Why is hantavirus so deadly? It's not what you may think
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report examines how Andes hantavirus causes severe lung failure and notes that its disease mechanism differs from other respiratory illnesses. Understanding that mechanism could inform future treatment approaches. The article is explanatory and mechanistic rather than a trial report.

articles.title: Congo prepared for Ebola. Now a rare strain is exposing gaps in readiness
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes how a Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in Congo is revealing weaknesses in outbreak readiness. It says responders are relying on traditional public health measures while new tools are sought. The piece focuses on preparedness and response capacity.

articles.title: Scientists discover a weak spot shared by polio and common cold viruses
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers described a shared replication vulnerability among enteroviruses, including pathogens linked to polio and the common cold. The work maps how viral RNA recruits viral and human proteins to control replication versus protein production. It is a basic science finding that could support future antiviral strategies.

articles.title: Engineered stem cells reverse new-onset type 1 diabetes in mice
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers developed an engineered stem cell therapy that reversed new-onset type 1 diabetes in a mouse model. The report presents this as a preclinical proof of concept. It does not show effectiveness in humans.

articles.title: Infections are major 'health hazard' for people with diabetes
articles.summary_3_sentences: A major study argues that infections in people with diabetes are an underrecognized source of illness, hospitalization, and death. The authors say current clinical guidelines do not adequately reflect this burden. The report frames infection risk as a broader diabetes management issue.

articles.title: GLP-1-based meds are linked to fewer heart events in adults with obesity, autoimmune disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: Among adults with obesity and autoimmune disease, GLP-1 receptor agonist use was associated with fewer serious cardiac events and fewer emergency department visits. The findings were presented as comparative observational research. They suggest potential cardiovascular benefit in a specific high-risk population.

articles.title: Scientists discover why ozempic may not work for some people
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified genetic variants linked to reduced response to GLP-1 drugs in type 2 diabetes. The report says roughly 10% of people may carry these variants and be less likely to reach glycemic targets. It points to a possible explanation for variable treatment response.

articles.title: Popular GLP-1 weight-loss drugs linked to lower risks of addiction and overdose
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large veterans study found GLP-1 drug use was associated with lower rates of several substance use disorders and fewer overdose-related outcomes. The article presents the findings as observational and hypothesis-generating. It suggests these drugs may have effects beyond weight and glucose control.

articles.title: This new diabetes pill burns fat without the downsides of Ozempic
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report describes an experimental diabetes and obesity pill that acts through skeletal muscle metabolism rather than appetite suppression. Early clinical results were described as safe and well tolerated. The proposed mechanism is distinct from GLP-1 drugs.

articles.title: Scientists discovered something surprising about french fries and diabetes
articles.summary_3_sentences: A long-term cohort study found that frequent french fry consumption was associated with higher risk of type 2 diabetes, while other potato preparations were not. Replacing potatoes with whole grains was linked to lower risk. The study is observational and addresses dietary associations, not direct causation.

articles.title: Omega-3 fish oil shows promise against type 2 diabetes
articles.summary_3_sentences: In diabetic rats, omega-3 supplementation improved blood sugar, lipids, and inflammatory markers. The report suggests the effect may involve shifting immune cells toward a less inflammatory state. These findings are preclinical and in an animal model.

articles.title: Intermittent fasting triggers surprising changes in the brain
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study in adults with obesity reported weight loss, metabolic improvement, microbiome changes, and altered brain activity during an intermittent fasting-style diet. The article suggests the gut and brain may jointly influence weight-loss success. It is presented as a human study linking metabolism, microbiome, and neural signals.

articles.title: This tomato-soy juice reduced inflammation in just four weeks
articles.summary_3_sentences: A clinical study in adults with obesity found that a tomato-soy juice reduced several inflammatory blood proteins over four weeks. A control tomato juice did not show the same effect. The report focuses on biomarker changes rather than long-term clinical outcomes.

articles.title: Scientists discover why Ozempic and Wegovy weight loss eventually plateaus
articles.summary_3_sentences: NIH researchers reported that semaglutide triggers different responses within appetite-regulating brain cells, which may help explain treatment plateaus. They also describe a possible way to extend drug effects. The work offers a mechanistic explanation for variable and waning weight-loss response.

articles.title: Popular GLP-1 weight-loss drugs like Ozempic slash heart attack and stroke risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large review found GLP-1 drugs were associated with lower risks of heart attack, stroke, heart failure, and premature death over time. The article presents these medicines as having cardiovascular effects beyond weight loss and diabetes control. It summarizes broader evidence rather than a single new trial.

articles.title: Screening all kids for type 1 diabetes can catch more cases early
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reports that broader screening can identify children with early signs of type 1 diabetes sooner. Earlier detection may help reduce severe presentation at diagnosis. The piece focuses on screening strategy and public health value.

articles.title: Newly discovered view of brain blood flow during surgery could prevent debilitation, save lives
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers report a new way to visualize brain blood flow during neurosurgery. The technique aims to help surgeons detect brief interruptions that can lead to major injury. The article emphasizes intraoperative monitoring potential.

articles.title: Researchers develop world's first AI for objective pain assessment
articles.summary_3_sentences: A research team developed an AI system that classifies pain intensity from EEG responses to thermal stimuli. The work aims to provide a more objective measure of pain. It is a technology-focused study rather than a routine clinical tool at present.

articles.title: Brain scans reveal two distinct types of autism
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers combined human brain imaging with genetically engineered mouse models and identified two autism-associated connectivity patterns. The report describes a hyperconnectivity subtype and a hypoconnectivity subtype. It suggests autism may include biologically distinct subgroups.

articles.title: Scientists reverse anxiety by fixing a tiny brain circuit
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study identified a small amygdala circuit involved in anxiety and social behavior. Restoring normal activity in that circuit reversed anxiety-like and social deficits in mice. The findings are preclinical and point to a possible future therapeutic target.

articles.title: The forgotten organ that could predict how long you live
articles.summary_3_sentences: Using AI analysis of CT scans, researchers found that healthier-appearing thymuses were associated with longer life and lower risks of major disease. The study ties an immune organ to aging outcomes in adults. It is an observational imaging-based finding.

articles.title: Scientists found the hidden switch fueling alzheimer's brain inflammation
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a chemical modification of STING that may keep brain immune activity overactive in Alzheimer's disease. The finding links this pathway to synaptic harm and neuroinflammation. It is a mechanistic discovery that may inform future therapies.

articles.title: Caffeine reversed memory problems caused by sleep deprivation
articles.summary_3_sentences: Laboratory research found that sleep deprivation disrupted a brain circuit involved in social memory. Caffeine restored signaling in that pathway and reversed the memory deficit in the study model. The work is preclinical and circuit-specific.

articles.title: Protein traffic jams may explain aging, memory loss, and Alzheimer's
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers studying killifish reported that ribosomes increasingly stall and collide with age, contributing to protein quality problems. The article links this process to age-related brain decline and protein aggregation relevant to Alzheimer's. It is a basic science study of aging biology.

articles.title: New depression treatment targets the immune system instead of the brain
articles.summary_3_sentences: A small clinical trial suggests an anti-inflammatory drug used in rheumatoid arthritis may help people with hard-to-treat depression. The report also notes improvements in fatigue, anxiety, and quality of life. Because the trial is small, the findings should be viewed as early evidence.

articles.title: CBD may slow Alzheimer's by calming the brain's immune system
articles.summary_3_sentences: In Alzheimer's mouse experiments, inhaled CBD reduced markers of neuroinflammation. The article suggests CBD may act by calming overactive brain immune responses. These are preclinical findings in mice, not evidence of clinical benefit in people.

articles.title: Human organoids reveal how to reverse "irreversible" nerve damage
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used human brain-and-spinal-cord organoid systems to study why neurons lose regenerative capacity during development. They identified a gene network involved and reported that an existing hormone drug boosted nerve fiber regrowth in the model. The work is preclinical and based on organoids.

articles.title: Researchers block key protein that helps Parkinson's spread through the brain
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified GPNMB as a protein that may help propagate Parkinson's-related damage between cells. Antibodies blocking GPNMB interrupted the process in early experiments. The findings are mechanistic and preclinical.

articles.title: Scientists thought brain inflammation was driving long COVID but the scans told a different story
articles.summary_3_sentences: A brain imaging study in people with long COVID found no evidence of widespread brain inflammation. Instead, worse symptoms were associated with increased activity in mood- and emotion-related regions. The findings refine hypotheses about neurological long COVID mechanisms.

articles.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers created vitamin K-based compounds that promoted neuronal differentiation more strongly than natural vitamin K in laboratory studies. The work is framed as a possible regenerative strategy for neurodegenerative disease. It remains preclinical and mechanistic.

articles.title: Popular anti-aging drug combo caused severe brain damage in mice
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study found that a drug combination explored for anti-aging caused severe myelin loss and brain changes. The affected cells resembled those seen in multiple sclerosis. These results are preclinical and raise safety concerns for that strategy.

articles.title: USC scientists discover a hidden Alzheimer's trigger and a possible way to shut it down
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified cPLA2 as a potential inflammatory driver in Alzheimer's disease, particularly relevant to APOE4-associated risk. They also described drug candidates that may reduce this inflammation. The report is mechanistic and early-stage.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden driver of aging -- Simple supplement reversed brain decline
articles.summary_3_sentences: In mice, declining Menin in the hypothalamus was linked to inflammation, bone loss, and cognitive decline. Restoring Menin or giving D-serine improved some aging-related changes. The work is preclinical and focused on aging mechanisms.

articles.title: Scientists found a hidden Alzheimer's trigger and shut it down
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified the enzyme IDOL as a possible Alzheimer's target. Removing it from neurons reduced amyloid plaque burden and improved brain-related measures in the reported study. This is preclinical research aimed at disease mechanisms.

articles.title: Scientists discover why Alzheimer's risk hits women so much harder
articles.summary_3_sentences: An analysis of more than 17,000 adults suggests women may be more sensitive to common dementia risk factors. The report argues that prevention strategies may need better sex-specific tailoring. It is an epidemiologic risk analysis rather than an intervention study.

articles.title: Scientists reverse Alzheimer's in mice with breakthrough nanotechnology
articles.summary_3_sentences: A nanotechnology approach restored amyloid clearance and blood-brain barrier function in Alzheimer's mice, according to the report. Treated older mice also showed improved behavior. The findings are preclinical and limited to mouse models.

articles.title: Scientists reversed memory loss by recharging the brain's tiny engines
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers developed a tool to boost mitochondrial activity in the brain and restored memory performance in mouse models of dementia. The study suggests neuronal energy failure may contribute directly to cognitive decline. It is a preclinical proof of concept.

articles.title: Scientists find hidden brain nutrient deficit that may fuel anxiety
articles.summary_3_sentences: A brain imaging analysis found lower choline levels in people with anxiety disorders, especially in the prefrontal cortex. The article presents this as a chemical signature associated with anxiety. It is observational and not a test of choline treatment.

articles.title: The brain's "feel good" chemical may be secretly fueling tinnitus
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study linked serotonin signaling to tinnitus-like behavior using light-based circuit manipulation. The finding may help explain why some people report worse tinnitus with SSRIs. It is a preclinical circuit study.

articles.title: New psychedelic-like drugs could treat depression without making you trip
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers created serotonin receptor-activating compounds that showed psychedelic-like pharmacology without hallucinatory-like behavior in animal tests. The work aims to separate therapeutic plasticity effects from psychedelic experience. It remains preclinical.

articles.title: Your "um" and pauses could reveal early dementia risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used AI analysis of natural speech to predict aspects of executive function. The report suggests everyday speech patterns could support early cognitive screening. It points toward a low-burden digital biomarker approach.

articles.title: This daily habit could lower dementia risk by 35%, scientists say
articles.summary_3_sentences: A long-term observational study linked drinking two to three cups of coffee daily with lower dementia risk, especially before age 75. The article notes the effect leveled off rather than increasing with more intake. This is an association study, not proof that coffee prevents dementia.

articles.title: Ultrasound aimed at the brain offers new hope for Parkinson's patients
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reports that high-intensity focused ultrasound relieved tremor, stiffness, and pain in Parkinson's disease. The treatment is noninvasive and aimed at the brain. The piece highlights therapeutic potential for symptom control.

articles.title: New tools may help diagnose Parkinson's earlier than ever
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article surveys emerging approaches for earlier Parkinson's diagnosis, including instrumented pens and chemical analyses such as earwax-based testing. It presents these methods as promising but still developing. The focus is on earlier detection rather than treatment.

topic_groups.topic: Gut Microbiome, Gastrointestinal and Nutrition Science

articles.title: Sweet Success: Licorice Compound Shows Promise for IBD
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reports on glycyrrhizin from black licorice as a promising candidate for inflammatory bowel disease. It presents the compound as a natural product under investigation rather than an established therapy. The focus is translational drug discovery for IBD.

articles.title: New Insights Into the Link Between Colon Cancer and a Bacterial Toxin
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report examines how a bacterial toxin may contribute to colon cancer biology. It highlights a mechanistic link between gut microbes and malignancy. The article is focused on underlying disease pathways rather than a clinical intervention.

articles.title: A good night's sleep begins with healthy gut bacteria. Here's how to look after yours
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article discusses the relationship between gut bacteria and sleep health. It frames gut care as one factor that may support better sleep, based on emerging research into the gut-sleep connection. The piece is explanatory and wellness-oriented rather than a new trial report.

articles.title: How mechanical stress can accelerate bone destruction in periodontitis
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used mouse models to show that excessive bite force worsened bone loss when periodontitis was also present. Gene expression analyses identified inflammatory pathways that were especially active in the combined condition. The work is preclinical and mechanistic.

articles.title: Lab-grown aging eye model reveals early AMD markers in weeks
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers developed a lab-grown aging eye model that rapidly shows early markers of age-related macular degeneration. The system centers on retinal pigment epithelium dysfunction and may help study disease onset. It is a preclinical disease-modeling advance.

articles.title: New noninvasive tool may allow early detection of dangerous intestinal disease in preemies
articles.summary_3_sentences: A first-in-human study suggests broadband optical spectroscopy could detect necrotizing enterocolitis earlier in premature infants. The article says the method may identify disease before changes appear on X-rays. This is an early diagnostic technology study.

articles.title: Clarifying the interplay between host and gut microbiota in selenium metabolism
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report explores how the body and gut microbiota jointly influence selenium metabolism. Because selenium has a narrow safe range, understanding these interactions may matter for nutrition and disease. The article is mechanistic and focuses on host-microbe metabolism.

articles.title: The supplements older adults actually need and the ones they don't
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article summarizes evidence-based guidance on supplements for older adults. It emphasizes that B12, vitamin D, and protein may be useful when deficiency or need is present, while unnecessary supplementation can cause harm or drug interactions. The piece is practical and cautionary rather than intervention-based.

articles.title: A study of 8,300 older adults revealed a surprising salt habit
articles.summary_3_sentences: A Brazilian study found that adding salt at the table remains common among older adults, especially men. The article links the habit to broader health concerns associated with excess sodium intake. It is an observational public health nutrition report.

articles.title: The biggest collagen study yet reveals what actually works
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review covering nearly 8,000 participants found that collagen supplements improved skin measures and osteoarthritis symptoms, with modest effects on muscle and tendon health. It reported little benefit for sports recovery or soreness. The findings synthesize heterogeneous supplement studies rather than establishing a universal effect size.

articles.title: Scientists discover gut bacteria that may help protect against autism and ADHD
articles.summary_3_sentences: This study links epigenetic patterns present at birth, early microbiome development, and later signs related to autism and ADHD. The article suggests that gene-microbe interactions may shape early neurodevelopmental trajectories. It is an associative developmental study, not a proof of protection or causation.

articles.title: This common amino acid helped mice survive deadly inflammation
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study found methionine improved survival during severe infection and inflammation. Rather than directly altering immunity, it appeared to improve kidney filtration and clearance of inflammatory molecules. The findings are preclinical and mechanistic.

articles.title: Vitamin B12 and folate deficiencies linked to chronic fatigue
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reported that lower vitamin B12 and folate levels were associated with fatigue and reduced motivation, even in otherwise healthy people. The article suggests nutrient deficiency may contribute to chronic tiredness in some cases. It is an observational association study.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden gut-brain circuit that triggers protein cravings
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a gut-brain signaling circuit that shifts animals toward seeking protein when protein is lacking. The study offers a mechanistic explanation for macronutrient-specific cravings. It is basic science research on appetite regulation.

articles.title: Scientists say guava juice could make iron supplements work better
articles.summary_3_sentences: A review of 17 studies found that guava juice, especially with iron supplements, was associated with improved hemoglobin levels in women and girls. The article attributes the effect to guava's high vitamin C content supporting iron absorption. It presents a low-cost nutritional strategy for anemia contexts.

articles.title: Scientists are raising new questions about vitamin B12 and cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reviews evidence suggesting that both low and very high vitamin B12 levels may be relevant to cancer risk or outcomes. It argues the relationship is more complex than simply treating B12 as uniformly beneficial. The piece is cautionary and interpretive rather than a single experimental result.

articles.title: Beet juice lowers blood pressure in older adults in just 2 weeks
articles.summary_3_sentences: In older adults, concentrated beetroot juice was associated with lower blood pressure after two weeks. The article suggests oral microbiome changes may be part of the mechanism. This is a short-term human nutrition study.

articles.title: Surprising research reveals why you shouldn't add bananas to your smoothies
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reported that adding bananas to berry smoothies reduced flavanol absorption. The finding highlights how food combinations can alter nutrient bioavailability. It is a nutrition mechanism study rather than a broader dietary trial.

articles.title: Scientists warn that current vitamin B12 guidelines may be putting your brain at risk
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study in older adults found that lower-normal active B12 levels were associated with slower cognition and more white matter damage. The article suggests current adequacy thresholds may miss some neurological vulnerability. It is an observational study about biomarker thresholds.

articles.title: Scientists uncover surprising health benefits of watermelon
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article summarizes studies suggesting that watermelon consumption is associated with higher diet quality and that watermelon juice may support vascular function. The evidence presented includes dietary pattern analyses and a juice study. It is suggestive nutrition research rather than a definitive intervention claim.

articles.title: This silent tooth infection could be hurting your whole body
articles.summary_3_sentences: The report links chronic tooth-root infections to systemic inflammation and poorer blood sugar control. It also notes studies in which root canal treatment was followed by improved glycemic measures and lower inflammation. The piece highlights oral-systemic health connections.

articles.title: New PET tracer identifies DVT in legs and lungs
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers presented a PET radiotracer that can visualize deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism in one whole-body scan. The article suggests it could streamline clot diagnosis in complex cases. This is an imaging innovation report presented at a scientific meeting.

articles.title: Can 'grip strength' exercises actually help you live longer?
articles.summary_3_sentences: This explainer examines claims that grip strength predicts longevity. It discusses grip strength as a marker associated with broader health rather than proof that hand exercises themselves extend life. The article is interpretive and evidence-focused.

articles.title: A silent kidney crisis is spreading far faster than experts expected
articles.summary_3_sentences: A global study found chronic kidney disease affects nearly 800 million people and is a leading cause of death. The article emphasizes that CKD is often silent early and contributes substantially to cardiovascular disease burden. It is a large-scale epidemiologic warning about underrecognized disease.

articles.title: Common heart drug taken by millions found useless -- and possibly dangerous
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large international study reported no clear benefit of beta blockers after uncomplicated heart attack when heart function remains normal. The article also notes a possible higher risk among women taking the drugs in this context. The findings question long-standing routine prescribing in a specific patient group.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden liver switch that cuts harmful cholesterol
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified HELZ2 as a regulator of apoB production in the liver, affecting cholesterol-carrying particles in the bloodstream. The finding provides a new mechanistic target relevant to atherosclerotic risk. It is basic science with translational potential.

articles.title: CAR-T cells enhanced with navigation system to penetrate lymph nodes more efficiently
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers modified CAR-T cells to improve their entry into lymph nodes, where lymphoma cells can persist. The strategy aims to address a major limitation in some patients who do not respond fully. The article describes a cell-engineering advance rather than a definitive clinical outcome study.

articles.title: Common arthritis drugs reduce systemic Sjogren's disease activity in 24 weeks
articles.summary_3_sentences: A clinical trial found that leflunomide plus hydroxychloroquine reduced systemic disease activity in Sjogren's disease over 24 weeks. The report says the combination was effective and had a favorable safety profile. It is presented as an affordable treatment option for moderate-to-severe systemic disease.

articles.title: This drug delayed rheumatoid arthritis for years after treatment ended
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that one year of abatacept delayed onset of rheumatoid arthritis in high-risk people by up to four years. The benefit reportedly persisted after treatment stopped. The study suggests prevention or delay of clinical RA may be possible in selected populations.

articles.title: Popular weight loss drugs like Wegovy may also target arthritis inflammation
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found very low levels of the GLP-1 hormone in arthritic joints and proposed that high-dose GLP-1 drugs might influence inflammation directly. The article presents a hypothesis-generating link between metabolic drugs and joint inflammation. It is mechanistic rather than a clinical efficacy trial in arthritis.

articles.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reported that leucine helps protect key mitochondrial proteins and enhances cellular energy production. The article suggests possible implications for metabolic disease and cancer biology. It is a basic science nutrition-metabolism finding.

articles.title: Mitochondria Regulates Immune Response
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article discusses how mitochondria influence immune responses beyond their role in cellular energy production. It highlights the organelle's importance in immunology and cell signaling. The piece is mechanistic and basic science oriented.

articles.title: Scientists discover the master clock that controls biological growth and development
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a genetic clock that orchestrates bursts of gene activity during worm development. Disrupting the clock halted development in the model. The study is basic developmental biology with possible relevance to growth disorders.

articles.title: Scientists discover inherited traits that break Mendel's Laws of genetics
articles.summary_3_sentences: A large mouse study found epigenetic inheritance patterns that do not follow classic Mendelian rules. The work includes evidence for naturally occurring paramutation in a mammal. It expands understanding of how traits can be transmitted across generations.

articles.title: Increased Activity From Transposons ID'ed in Neurodegeneration
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report links increased transposon activity to neurodegeneration. It focuses on how mobile genetic elements may contribute to disease-related genomic instability or dysfunction. The article is mechanistic and genetics-focused.

articles.title: The Evolution of Blood Cells
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article explores how blood cells evolved and how they arise from bone marrow in modern organisms. It is a broad biology-oriented overview of hematopoietic evolution. The piece is basic science rather than a disease report.

articles.title: Scientists discover ancient single-celled ancestors still live on in your blood
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reconstructed the evolutionary history of blood cells and traced aspects of them back to ancient single-celled ancestors. The work connects modern immune cell biology to deep evolutionary origins. It is a basic evolutionary biology study.

articles.title: Epigenetics Break the Bounds of Mendelian Inheritance
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article covers research on epigenetic inheritance that can depart from standard Mendelian expectations. It highlights chemical modifications and transmission patterns beyond DNA sequence alone. The focus is genetics and heredity mechanisms.

articles.title: Scientists discover why some DNA-doubled cells refuse to die
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers studied cells that duplicate their DNA but fail to divide, leaving them with extra genetic material. The work shows that these errors are not all biologically equivalent and may have different consequences for disease and aging. It is a cell biology and genome stability study.

articles.title: Surprising Discovery Reveals DNA Transfer Between Human Cells
articles.summary_3_sentences: This report describes evidence for DNA transfer between human cells. The finding challenges simple assumptions about how genetic material is confined within cells. It is a basic science study of cellular communication and genetics.

articles.title: Novel CRISPR Technique Aims to Destroy Virus-Infected Cells
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article describes a CRISPR-based strategy designed to selectively destroy virus-infected cells. The work represents an engineering extension of gene-editing tools. It is an early-stage translational genetics approach.

articles.title: Scientists think they've cracked the mystery of human right-handedness
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers propose that bipedalism and larger brains helped drive the strong right-hand preference seen in humans. The study reframes handedness as the product of major evolutionary transitions. It is an evolutionary explanation rather than a medical finding.

articles.title: Scientists found the "holy grail" gene that could one day help humans regrow limbs
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers studying axolotls, zebrafish, and mice identified regeneration-related genes important for bone regrowth. They also used a zebrafish-inspired gene therapy to partially restore regeneration in mice. The work is preclinical and focused on regenerative biology.

topic_groups.topic: Public Health, Health Systems and Risk

articles.title: Gap widens between expected help and municipal care delivered, complaint review shows
articles.summary_3_sentences: A complaint review found a mismatch between what patients expect and what municipal health services deliver. The analysis is based on complaints submitted to a patient ombudsman. It highlights patient experience and care delivery gaps in local health systems.

articles.title: Poor sleep threatens health, society and the economy
articles.summary_3_sentences: An international team argues that poor sleep should be treated as a global health priority rather than just an individual issue. The article emphasizes health, social, and economic costs of widespread sleep problems. It is a policy-oriented perspective on sleep health.

articles.title: Integrated care may cut ER visits and hospital stays for adults with developmental disabilities
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that adults with developmental disabilities receiving integrated care were less likely to visit the emergency room or be hospitalized. The findings suggest care coordination may improve outcomes in this population. The study is health services research.

articles.title: Cohort makes data from more than 5,800 patients hospitalized with COVID-19 available to the scientific community
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers published a COVID-19 hospitalization dataset covering more than 5,800 patients to support reuse by the scientific community. The article is about data sharing and research infrastructure. It may help future clinical and epidemiological analyses.

articles.title: Fungus threatens food and human health, researchers argue
articles.summary_3_sentences: Experts warn that agricultural chemical use may be contributing to fungal threats affecting both food systems and human health. The article links farm practices to the emergence of harder-to-treat infections. It is a public health warning focused on antimicrobial resistance and fungi.

articles.title: How house design can curb childhood illnesses in Africa
articles.summary_3_sentences: Experimental housing designs in Tanzania reduced malaria, diarrhea, and infections in children. Features included screening, rainwater systems, and better ventilation. The article highlights housing as a public health intervention.

articles.title: Common pesticide linked to hidden brain damage, scientists warn
articles.summary_3_sentences: A study in children linked prenatal chlorpyrifos exposure to later brain abnormalities and poorer motor function. The article frames this as evidence of developmental neurotoxicity from a common pesticide. It is an environmental health report with long-term exposure concerns.

articles.title: Problematic Cannabis Use is Common Among College Students
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reports that problematic cannabis use and related harms are common among college students. It draws on a study in the Journal of Cannabis Research. The focus is on prevalence and student health risk.

articles.title: Hidden driving danger when edible cannabis and alcohol mix
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that combining edible cannabis with alcohol led to greater driving impairment than either alone. The article also notes that standard field sobriety tests often missed cannabis-related impairment. It has implications for road safety and enforcement.

articles.title: Is cannabis safe after 65? Stanford experts reveal 5 risks older adults should know
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article outlines health risks of cannabis use in older adults, including falls, cardiovascular issues, memory problems, interactions, and addiction. It emphasizes that modern cannabis products are often much more potent than in the past. The piece is a risk-focused expert summary.

articles.title: A Natural Compound That May Disarm Drug-Resistant Bacteria
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article describes a natural compound being studied for activity against drug-resistant bacteria. It presents the work as part of ongoing efforts to overcome antimicrobial resistance. The report is early-stage and mechanism-oriented.

articles.title: Ancient Chinese medicine could transform hair loss treatment
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reviews evidence that Polygonum multiflorum may affect pathways involved in hair loss. Reported actions include hormone-related effects, follicle protection, and blood-flow support. The piece is translational and exploratory rather than a definitive clinical trial report.

articles.title: Scientists are seriously asking if bees and ChatGPT are conscious
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article discusses new thinking on consciousness in animals and artificial systems. It argues that behavior alone may be insufficient and that internal mechanisms matter more for assessment. The piece is conceptual science news rather than biomedicine.

articles.title: Beluga whales keep switching mates and it may be saving their species
articles.summary_3_sentences: DNA analysis of belugas in Alaska revealed a flexible mating system with multiple partners over time for both sexes. The report suggests this may support population resilience. It is wildlife biology news rather than medical research.

articles.title: A hidden pollutant is changing how the world's forests breathe
articles.summary_3_sentences: A global analysis found that nitrogen pollution can alter forest soil respiration in different ways depending on ecosystem condition. The results suggest hidden tipping points relevant to carbon storage and climate resilience. This is environmental science news.

articles.title: Your brain starts making social decisions before you do
articles.summary_3_sentences: In zebrafish, researchers observed coordinated brain activity before visible social approach behavior. The pallium appeared important, and stronger signals were associated with greater sociability. The study is basic neuroscience in an animal model.

articles.title: Revealing the Secrets of a Bacterial Enzyme in a Quest for New Antibiotics
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article describes research into a bacterial enzyme as part of efforts to develop new antibiotics. It focuses on mechanistic understanding that could support future drug design. The report is basic microbiology with translational relevance.

articles.title: The ocean's health may depend on a tiny microbe inside fish
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found evidence that microbes in marine fish guts help produce calcium carbonate important to ocean chemistry. The study reveals a host-microbe partnership affecting carbon storage processes. It is environmental and marine science research.

articles.title: The secret to pigeons' incredible navigation was hiding in their liver
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified iron-rich immune cells in pigeon livers that may function as magnetic sensors. Birds lacking these cells were less able to navigate under overcast conditions. The work links immunity and magnetoreception in an animal model.

articles.title: A 100-year-old piano mystery has finally been solved
articles.summary_3_sentences: Using high-speed sensing of piano key motion, researchers found that skilled performers can alter tone color in ways listeners can detect. The work addresses a long-standing question in music science. It is a sensory and performance study outside medicine.

articles.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles' lost crocodiles
articles.summary_3_sentences: DNA from museum specimens showed the Seychelles' lost crocodiles were an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles rather than a separate species. The findings resolve a long-standing taxonomic mystery. It is evolutionary and conservation science news.

articles.title: Forget LASIK: Safer, cheaper vision correction without lasers or surgery
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers are testing an alternative vision-correction approach that reshapes the cornea using electrical pulses and contact lenses. Early rabbit-eye experiments corrected nearsightedness without incisions or laser treatment. The work is preclinical and device-oriented.

articles.title: New drug could finally stop deadly fatty liver disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: An experimental drug called ION224 was reported to improve liver measures in people with MASH. The article says it targets an enzyme involved in fat buildup and inflammation and showed benefits even without weight loss. It describes a clinical-development advance for fatty liver disease.

articles.title: New MIND Tool Analyzes Microbiomes to Understand & Alter Them
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article introduces a tool designed to analyze microbiomes and potentially support ways to modify them. It focuses on computational or systems-level understanding of microbial communities. The report is methodological and translational.

articles.title: Venomous Himalayan pit viper was actually 5 different species all along
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers found that the Himalayan pit viper actually comprises five separate species, including three newly recognized ones. The discovery revises biodiversity understanding in the region. It is taxonomy and evolutionary biology news.

articles.title: Evaluating Spatial Transcriptomics Platforms for IBD Clinics & Research
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reviews or compares spatial transcriptomics platforms relevant to IBD research and clinical applications. It focuses on how tissue-level gene-expression mapping may aid understanding of inflammatory bowel disease. The report is methodological and translational.

articles.title: Oxygenated Seas Hosted Early Complex Life-Findings Challenge Assumptions
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article reports research suggesting early complex life arose in oxygenated marine settings, challenging prior assumptions. It concerns the environmental conditions of ancient eukaryotic evolution. The piece is earth and life science news.

articles.title: New Dry Eye Treatment Makes Eyes Photosynthesize
articles.summary_3_sentences: This article describes an experimental dry-eye treatment inspired by photosynthesis. The concept appears to use light-driven biological processes to support eye surface health. The work is unusual, early-stage, and translational.

articles.title: AI scans 400,000 Reddit posts and finds hidden Ozempic side effects
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers used AI to analyze Reddit posts and identify side effects discussed by GLP-1 drug users that may be undercaptured in trials. Reported themes included menstrual changes, chills, and hot flashes. The work suggests social media may supplement pharmacovigilance, though such data can be noisy and self-reported.

articles.title: Wegovy linked to rare "eye stroke" that can cause sudden blindness
articles.summary_3_sentences: An analysis of FDA adverse-event reports found a strong safety signal linking Wegovy to a rare ischemic eye event. The article notes that this type of database can identify signals but does not prove causation. It raises a need for closer investigation of potential risk.

articles.title: Scientists discover simple way to relieve arthritis pain without pills or surgery
articles.summary_3_sentences: A year-long clinical trial found that changing foot angle during walking reduced knee osteoarthritis pain and slowed cartilage damage. The article compares the effect to common medications. It presents a nonpharmacologic biomechanical intervention for knee OA.

articles.title: Childhood junk food may rewire the brain for life
articles.summary_3_sentences: Animal research found that high-fat, high-sugar diets early in life altered feeding behavior and brain appetite circuits into adulthood. The report also says certain bacteria and prebiotic fibers helped reverse some changes. These findings are preclinical and focused on brain-gut effects of diet.

articles.title: The real reason exercise makes you stronger isn't what you think
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study found that certain brain cells remained active after exercise and appeared necessary for improved endurance. Blocking those neurons prevented stamina gains despite continued exercise. The work suggests central nervous system contributions to training adaptation.

articles.title: Scientists discover tiny gut particles that may drive aging and chronic disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers reported that microscopic gut-derived particles may contribute to inflammation and chronic disease with aging. In the study, particles from young animals appeared to counter some damage in older animals. The findings are preclinical and exploratory.

articles.title: A grad student's wild idea sparks a major aging breakthrough
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers developed aptamers that bind selectively to senescent cells. The method could help identify and target harmful senescent cells in living tissues with greater precision. It is a platform advance in aging biology and cell targeting.

articles.title: Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists reported detecting collagen remnants in a 66-million-year-old dinosaur fossil using advanced analytical methods. The finding challenges assumptions that fossilization destroys all original organic material. It is paleontology and molecular preservation research.

articles.title: Giant squid discovery uncovers a hidden deep-sea world off Australia
articles.summary_3_sentences: Environmental DNA from deep waters off Australia revealed a rich and unusual marine community, including evidence of giant squid. The study identified many species from deep-diving whales to rare fish, with some possibly undescribed. It is biodiversity and ocean science news.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden fat-burning switch that could strengthen bones
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers identified a glycerol-triggered pathway in brown fat that supports heat production and metabolism. The article also suggests possible links to bone health. It is a mechanistic metabolic biology study.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden chemical signature that could reveal alien life
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers propose that life may be detectable through statistical patterns in amino acids and fatty acids rather than specific molecules alone. The work defines a chemical signature that differentiates living from nonliving systems. It is astrobiology and analytical science news.

articles.title: Scientists successfully transfer longevity gene and extend lifespan
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers transferred a naked mole rat longevity-related gene into mice and reported longer lifespan and improved health. The work links high-molecular-weight hyaluronic acid to healthier aging and reduced inflammation. These are preclinical findings in genetically modified mice.

excluded_items.title: Black licorice compound shows promise against inflammatory bowel disease
New chemical kills 95% of termites without harming humans
Baby Microbiomes are Nourished by Breast Milk Sugars
Updates on the Growing Problem of Fungal Infections
How the Global Wildlife Trade Harms Humans
How One Cancer Care Center Used CLSI AUTO15 for Smarter Autoverification
Moderate Cannabis Use Has No Impact on Adult Brains
Novel Approach Engineers Bacteria to Deliver Viruses for Cancer Treatment
Specific Immune Cells Found to Target a Range of Viral Strains
CBD Shows Early Promise for Alzheimer's
Cyanobacteria Find Another Use for DNA
